steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question tests punctuation and sentence structure. The sentence has two independent clauses: (1) "The Kyzylkum Desert in central Asia is a semiarid desert, for example" and (2) "With a total area of about 300,000 km², it is also one of the largest deserts of any type." Two independent clauses need to be separated by a period, semicolon, or coordinating conjunction — not just a comma.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — example with: Joins both clauses with no punctuation between them, creating a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — example and with: Uses "and" to connect two independent clauses, but without a comma before "and," this is still a comma splice issue, and "and with" produces an awkward construction. ✗
  • Choice C — example, with: Places only a comma between two independent clauses, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — example. With: A period correctly ends the first independent clause. "With a total area of about 300,000 km², it is also one of the largest deserts of any type" then begins a new, complete sentence. ✓
That matches Choice D — example. With.
